summary.intent: Build a live Expo web dashboard fed by a local Express+WebSocket server to display Claude hook-handler sessions and events from SQLite, then improve the UI and link plan sessions to their implementation sessions.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the API server, Expo web app, session linking heuristic, and UI fixes (column alignment, user chip, keyword chips, parent-session banner). All components verified working end-to-end.

repo: gossamer uses NodeNext module resolution — all local imports in src/ must use .js extensions
handler.ts uses dynamic import for server.ts so express/ws are not loaded on every hook invocation
Sessions already have a parentSessionId field used for subagents; plan→implementation linking reuses the same agentParents mechanism
reconstruct command replays all raw events and can be used to backfill derived fields like parentSessionId

Claude Code fires UserPromptSubmit for subsequent messages but NOT for the first message of a session that was started by approving a plan — the plan session ends and a new session starts with SessionStart then immediately PreToolUse

code.finding: Exports COL_WIDTHS constants so the header in ActiveSessions.tsx uses identical widths, fixing column misalignment between header and rows
subscribeToUpdates uses WebSocket with exponential backoff: 1s → 2s → ... → 30s cap; returns an unsubscribe function that closes the WS and cancels any pending reconnect timer

WebSocketServer shares port 3000 with Express by passing httpServer to its constructor; polls db.event.findFirst({ orderBy: { id: 'desc' } }) every 2s and broadcasts sessions_updated when max id changes

Plan→implementation linking: on SessionStart, queries for a SessionEnd in the same cwd within the last 30 seconds; if found, sets parentSessionId to link the sessions. Same logic applied in runReconstruct using event timestamps.

workflow: Expo 52 requires react@18.3.1 — 18.3.2 does not exist on npm despite being commonly cited
@expo/metro-runtime must be explicitly installed for Expo web support; it is not pulled in automatically
npm install inside app/ fails if the parent node_modules has a partial/hoisted react; --legacy-peer-deps works around this
npx expo start --web serves on port 19006 and hot-reloads on file changes without restarting

friction: Plan specified react@18.3.2 which does not exist; caused npm install failure
npm install conflicted with hoisted react from parent node_modules, requiring --legacy-peer-deps
@expo/metro-runtime was not included in the plan but is required for Expo web — discovered only at startup
react-native version in plan (0.76.3) did not match Expo 52's expected version (0.76.9), causing a peer dep warning
Port 3000 already in use during smoke-testing because a prior background server process was not fully cleaned up
Claude Code never fires UserPromptSubmit for the first message of an implementation session started by plan approval — this is a hard platform limitation with no workaround
open_items: The 30-second same-cwd heuristic for plan→implementation linking could produce false positives if two unrelated sessions start in the same directory within that window
Sessions started via plan approval will always have an empty Summary until the user sends a follow-up message — no hook captures the initial prompt
The Expo app uses hardcoded localhost URLs; would need configuration for use on a real device or remote host
No authentication or origin restriction on the API server beyond cors() with no restrictions — acceptable for a local dev tool but worth noting
